title: Performance, bundle, and error hardening
summary: Make the source-complete alpha build resilient on fresh machines and browsers.

lane_1_scout_refinement.result: Narrowed lane 1 to repo-local ignore coverage for review-drop scratch workspaces.
main_scope: Add .tmp to the repo .gitignore because review:verify intentionally extracts and installs there.
Add a focused review-drop hygiene test that protects the local/generated folder ignore list.
Keep review-drop scripts, package scripts, README wording, runtime source, and UI behavior unchanged unless the guard exposes a mismatch.
out_of_scope: Full clean-extract verification requirement for this tiny main pass
Runtime code, save schema, route behavior, station behavior, content, geometry, or browser UI changes

lane_1_main_result.result: Repo-local ignore coverage now protects review-drop clean-extract scratch workspaces.
behavior_notes: .tmp is now ignored by the project instead of relying on a personal global gitignore.
The new test guards the generated/local folder ignore list used by build and review-drop workflows.
Review-drop scripts, package scripts, README wording, runtime source, and UI behavior were not changed.
critic_focus: Confirm .tmp is present in .gitignore alongside the existing generated folders.
Confirm the new test uses Vite raw import rather than Node fs/path types so build remains clean.
Confirm no review-drop script, package script, README, runtime, route, save, station, content, geometry, or UI drift landed.

lane_1_critic_result.result: No blocker found; review-drop scratch ignore coverage is scoped and verified.
notes: .tmp is now project-ignored alongside existing generated/local folders.
The new test uses Vite raw import support instead of Node fs/path types.
Review-drop scripts, package scripts, README wording, runtime code, route behavior, save schema, station behavior, content, geometry, and UI were unchanged.

lane_4_scout_refinement.result: Narrowed lane 4 to a browser-proof pass across seeded Route v2 loop states, with console/page error capture and no planned runtime changes.
main_scope: Run a real browser proof at the handheld viewport baseline using the debug save snapshots for high-pass-active, high-pass-ready-to-file, and high-pass-filed.
Capture PNG plus render_game_to_text JSON or equivalent state for each seeded route state under output/lane-4-main-425-browser/.
Capture console/page errors to output/lane-4-main-425-browser/console-errors.json even when empty.
Summarize active/replay labels, ready station filing state, and filed route suppression in docs/reports/2026-04-20-route-loop-browser-proof-implementation.md.
out_of_scope: New route types, support ids, station pages, inventory/loadout UI, content density, geometry, or save fields.
Broad station chrome, spatial frame, science-copy, or route-definition rewrites.
Runtime changes unless the browser proof exposes a real lane-4 route/support/replay blocker.
recommended_checks: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "files High Pass from the live talus-hold loop and settles the completed field arc"

lane_4_main_result.result: Completed the lane-4 browser proof across seeded High Pass active, ready-to-file, filed, and replay-facing surfaces with no runtime changes.
behavior_notes: Active High Pass shows Rimed Pass in the journal and Today: Rimed Pass on the world map.
Ready-to-file High Pass keeps Ready To File route state while the world-map replay and marker are cleared, then reaches the station NOTE card through the world map.
Filed High Pass clears active request, world-map marker, world-map replay, and journal request while keeping the station FILED card.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "files High Pass from the live talus-hold loop and settles the completed field arc"
shared web-game client boot smoke against production preview
one-off Playwright seeded snapshot proof against production preview
visual inspection of active world-map, ready station, filed world-map, and filed station screenshots
critic_focus: Confirm no runtime, route-definition, station-shell, save-schema, content, or geometry files changed for this proof item.
Confirm the browser artifacts include active, ready-to-file, and filed states plus empty console/page error capture.
Confirm the first transition-capture false start was harness-only and the final proof waits until world-map transition completion before asserting replay labels.

lane_4_critic_result.result: No lane-4 blocker found in the route-loop browser proof.
verification: Visually inspected active world-map, ready station, filed world-map, and filed station screenshots.
notes: Active High Pass carries Rimed Pass into journal and world-map replay surfaces.
Ready-to-file High Pass clears world-map replay/marker state and reaches the station NOTE filing card through the world map.
Filed High Pass clears active request, world-map marker, world-map replay, and journal request while preserving the station FILED card.
The initial transition-capture mismatch was harness-only; the final proof waits until world-map transition completion before asserting replay labels.
No runtime, route-definition, station-shell, save-schema, content, or geometry files changed for this proof item.

lane_3_scout_refinement.result: Narrowed lane 3 to a proof-first browser pass across representative spatial frames with console/page error capture.
main_scope: Capture ignored browser screenshots and render_game_to_text JSON under output/lane-3-main-424-spatial-proof/.
Seed first-session, forest-moisture-holders, high-pass-active, and tundra-thaw-window from window.get_debug_save_snapshots(), then capture the corresponding field views.
Optionally add deeper forest-cave-trunk, treeline-rime-brow or treeline-talus-hold, and tundra-thaw-bench frames if the traversal harness remains stable.
Summarize screenshots, JSON state, console/page errors, visual inspection, and whether any lane-3 rendering or asset blocker was found.
out_of_scope: route-state, support-choice, station, save-schema, journal, content-fact, or authored-copy changes
new screenshot framework or committed output artifacts
new geometry or asset edits unless browser proof exposes a concrete lane-3 blocker
lane-4 route-loop assertions, which are already covered by ECO-20260420-main-425
baseline_checks: Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "debug save snapshots|High Pass|tundra|treeline"
Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/treeline-biome.test.ts src/test/tundra-biome.test.ts -t "Root Hollow|root-hollow|High Pass|talus|stone|canopy|cave|thaw|frost"

lane_3_main_result.result: Completed the lane-3 browser proof across representative seeded spatial frames with no runtime changes.
behavior_notes: The seeded beach, forest, treeline/high-country, and tundra field frames render as playing biome scenes with expected biome ids and no blocking overlays.
The proof recorded 20 passing assertions and an empty console/page error capture.
Optional deeper traversal frames were not used for the pass because the quick browser movement path did not land on exact manifest frame bands.
scope_notes: No runtime source, route state, support choice, station, save schema, journal, content fact, authored copy, geometry, or asset edits landed.
Generated browser screenshots/state stay under ignored output/.
verification: Passed: shared web-game client boot smoke against production preview
Passed: one-off Playwright seeded snapshot proof against production preview
Passed: visual inspection of first-session beach, forest moisture holders, high-pass active treeline, and tundra thaw-window screenshots
Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "debug save snapshots|High Pass|tundra|treeline"
Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/treeline-biome.test.ts src/test/tundra-biome.test.ts -t "Root Hollow|root-hollow|High Pass|talus|stone|canopy|cave|thaw|frost"

lane_3_critic_result.result: No lane-3 blocker found in the representative spatial browser proof.
verification: Visually inspected first-session beach, forest moisture holders, high-pass active treeline, and tundra thaw-window screenshots.
Reviewed optional-notes.json and confirmed optional deeper traversal misses were documented as non-pass evidence.
notes: The required seeded beach, forest, treeline/high-country, and tundra field frames render as playing biome scenes with expected biome ids and no blocking overlays.
No runtime source, route state, support choice, station, save schema, journal, content fact, authored copy, geometry, or asset change was needed for this proof item.
The mixed working tree contains unrelated dirty files from other lane work, so lane-clear commit/push is not recommended from this workspace.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: Build/test/validate pass from a clean install, browser console proof is clean on core alpha states, and review archives are source-complete.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Own clean install, build/test/validate, package ignore, script docs, and any low-risk runtime error fixes.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Check that content volume or copy helpers are not driving unnecessary bundle/runtime cost.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Run browser proof across representative spatial frames and flag rendering or asset regressions.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Run route-loop browser proof across active/ready/filed/replay surfaces and capture console errors.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.
